title: Zhangjiajie Grand Canyon
text: A separate location 35 minutes from the national park, home to the famous glass bridge. Canyon + glass bridge combo ticket is 219 yuan. The route descends via 3,000+ steps past waterfalls, caves and lakes, with a boat ride on an underground river.

title: Tianmen Fox Fairy night show
text: A grand open-air musical at the foot of Tianmen Mountain, one of China's most ambitious performances. Based on the legend of a fox spirit who fell in love with a mortal - music, dance, acrobatics and special effects against real mountains and waterfalls. 90 min, 228-888 yuan.

title: Food in Zhangjiajie - where and what to eat
text: Park food is overpriced and mediocre - bring snacks. Wulingyuan has dozens of restaurants. Try Sanxiaguo (three-pot stew), smoked pork, rice noodles and spicy tofu. Hunan cuisine is VERY spicy - say bu yao la (not spicy) if you do not love heat.

title: What to pack for Zhangjiajie park
text: Essentials: comfortable non-slip shoes (trails get wet), a raincoat (not umbrella - useless on narrow paths), 1.5-2 liters of water, power bank and sunscreen. Useful extras: gloves in winter, snacks, offline maps and motion sickness pills for bus serpentines.

title: Which entrance: Wulingyuan or Forest Park?
text: The park has two main entrances. Forest Park leads straight to Golden Whip Stream and scenic forest paths, but has the biggest morning queues. Wulingyuan offers quick access to the Bailong Elevator and less congestion - ideal to head straight up to the Avatar Mountains.
